reforms.description: The UK government has introduced the Pathways to Work programme, which includes a Work Capability Assessment to evaluate the capacity of disability benefits claimants to participate in the workforce, aiming to reduce the number of individuals on disability benefits.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The programme aims to enhance workforce participation, thereby increasing human capital and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been actively in place since its introduction.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, the reform is not system-wide but focuses on a specific aspect of workforce participation.
reforms.source_quote: The Pathways to Work programme was successfully trailed and is now being rolled out across the country.

reforms.description: The UK government has initiated a planning reform programme following the Planning Act 2008, which aims to streamline the process for major national infrastructure projects, reducing the time from application to decision.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving the efficiency of infrastructure project approvals, the reform supports economic growth and development.
reforms.status_evidence: The planning reform programme has been actively implemented since its initiation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of the planning process for national infrastructure.
reforms.source_quote: The Planning Act 2008 will set up a new Infrastructure Planning Commission, to be in place later this year, which will put in place a faster, more certain and transparent process for planning for major national infrastructure projects.

reforms.description: The introduction of Payment by Results (PbR) is a significant reform in the NHS that establishes an activity-based funding mechanism for hospital care, rewarding providers based on output activity rather than quality of outcomes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This reform incentivizes efficiency and productivity in healthcare delivery, potentially improving health outcomes and economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The PbR system has been in operation and is actively used in the NHS.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a major shift in how NHS funding is allocated and managed.
reforms.source_quote: The introduction of an activity-based funding mechanism for reimbursement of providers for hospital care has been a key element of the NHS reforms.
